meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't validate ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't validate ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Template: Validate SaaS Ideas Fast for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to validate SaaS ideas in hours, not weeks. Beginner-friendly guide.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template eliminates the 'validation paralysis' by providing a pre-configured, production-ready scaffold. By pairing it with Claude Code, you automate boilerplate setup and backend logic, allowing you to deploy a functional MVP in under two hours. This approach transforms abstract ideas into testable, high-fidelity prototypes immediately.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, your client’s deadline looming, and that nagging voice in your head asks: 'What if nobody actually wants this?' The graveyard of failed SaaS projects is filled with developers who spent months perfecting code for a product that never found a market. You don't have the luxury of time to build in a vacuum.

True validation isn't about writing perfect code; it's about speed to feedback. The Antigravity template provides the structural scaffolding you need to bypass the 'setup tax' of new projects. When you combine this with the autonomous capabilities of Claude Code, you stop being a developer stuck in configuration hell and start being an architect who ships solutions that actually solve problems.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: The foundation matters. We pull the template to gain pre-configured authentication, database schemas, and API routes that are industry-standard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity SaaS boilerplate with Supabase integration and Tailwind CSS styling.

steps.title: Define the Core Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing logic, we define the MVP scope. This prevents scope creep and ensures the code directly addresses the user's core problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core value prop: a tool that scrapes LinkedIn profiles for lead generation. Generate a schema for leads and user credits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create database schema for leads, users, and usage_logs'

steps.title: Automate the API Layer
steps.description: Manual boilerplate for CRUD operations is a waste of time. We use Claude to scaffold the backend endpoints based on our new schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Next.js API routes for the lead generation tool, ensuring secure authentication and rate limiting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate secure CRUD API routes for leads using Next.js app router'

steps.title: Integrate the Validation Logic
steps.description: We build the 'hook'—the part of the app that proves value—by connecting the frontend to the backend logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive dashboard component that allows users to input a profile URL and trigger the lead scraper function.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create dashboard UI with lead input form and status tracker'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Shipping is the final validation. We move from local code to a live URL so real users can interact with the product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Prepare the project for production deployment on Vercel, including environment variable configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure production build settings and output deployment commands'

pro_tips: Always use the '--dry-run' flag in Claude Code to review proposed file changes before committing to complex template modifications.
Treat the Antigravity template as a living document; keep your 'prompts.txt' file inside the repo to track every architectural decision you make.
Prioritize 'throwaway code' for the first version—if a feature isn't core to the validation, don't build it yet.
Use Claude Code's 'review' feature after every major file generation to catch potential security vulnerabilities in your auth boilerplate.
Integrate a simple feedback collection widget immediately; if you aren't collecting user email addresses, you aren't validating, you're just coding.
content.next_steps: Now that you have a live MVP, share the link with 10 potential users in your niche. Collect their feedback, iterate on the core feature using Claude Code, and pivot if the data shows low engagement.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping products that users actually crave. Our community of high-level AI builders is waiting to help you scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
